items.q: What are IV drips at home in Dubai?
items.a: IV drips at home are medically supervised infusions of fluids, vitamins, minerals, antioxidants. They are used to support hydration, energy, immunity, skin radiance and recovery, depending on the drip chosen.

items.q: Is IV therapy safe?
items.a: When carried out by licensed medical professionals with proper screening, IV therapy is generally considered safe. At NADZ, we review your health history and current condition before recommending a drip, and we will not proceed if we feel it is not appropriate or safe for you.

items.q: What is glutathione IV therapy?

items.q: How long is a glutathione IV session?
items.a: Most sessions take 30–45 minutes, depending on your protocol and comfort.

items.q: How many sessions are needed?
items.a: It depends on your goal, starting point, and how your body responds. Your clinician can suggest a plan after reviewing your history and expectations.

items.a: Many people choose glutathione IV therapy to support a brighter-looking, more even-toned complexion. Outcomes vary, and your clinician will guide what's realistic and appropriate.

items.q: Is glutathione IV therapy safe?

items.q: Can I get glutathione IV therapy at my hotel or office in Dubai?
items.a: Yes, subject to hotel policies and suitability of the room or setup. We can deliver care at home, a hotel, or an office for maximum convenience.

items.q: Is this service available 24/7?
items.a: Yes. Our coordination team is available 24/7, and we do our best to schedule sessions at a time that works for you.

items.q: Do I need lab tests before glutathione IV therapy?
items.a: For many wellness-focused sessions, a detailed medical history may be sufficient. If your clinician feels labs are needed, NADZ can arrange testing at home.

items.a: With NADZ, you're not booking a generic "wellness drip." You're receiving medically guided glutathione IV therapy delivered at home by DHA-licensed clinicians, with hospital-grade safety, comfort-first service, and seamless coordination with other NADZ services.

centeredText.title: What is Brain Mapping (QEEG)?
paragraphs: Brain mapping, also known as QEEG (Quantitative Electroencephalography), is a non-invasive test that records your brain's electrical activity and converts it into data patterns that can be reviewed by specialists.
QEEG brain mapping can help clinicians understand how the brain is functioning, often supporting assessment and monitoring in cases where symptoms feel "real" but hard to explain.
Note: QEEG supports clinical decision-making but is not a standalone diagnosis. Your results are always interpreted in context.

centeredText2.title: Your Brain Holds the Answers. We Help You Read Them.
paragraphs: At NADZ Home Healthcare, we see the brain as the control centre of how you think, feel, focus, sleep, and function every day. NADZ Vital Brain is our exclusive service that combines brain mapping at home in Dubai using quantitative EEG (QEEG) with AI-supported insights to understand brain performance with clinical-grade precision.

Clinical clarity for neurological and psychiatric concerns
Integrative, long-term optimisation (brain + gut + hormones + nutrition), based on your package

items.a: Brain mapping (QEEG) is an advanced EEG-based assessment that measures brainwave activity and helps build a data-driven view of brain performance and patterns.

items.q: Who should consider NADZ Vital Brain?
items.a: People experiencing anxiety, mood changes, sleep issues, brain fog, memory concerns, migraines, cognitive decline concerns, or those looking to optimise focus and performance.

items.q: How is the test done at home?
items.a: Our team sets up a non-invasive EEG recording in your space, runs required cognitive assessments (package-based), and completes any add-on screenings included in your plan.

items.q: Is Vital Brain only for diagnosis?
items.a: No. It supports both clinical assessment/tracking and wellness optimisation (especially in Elite/Platinum packages that connect brain health with blood markers, genetics, and gut-brain insights).

items.q: How long does testing take?
items.a: Timing varies by package and the number of assessments included. Your coordinator will confirm the expected duration when you book.

items.q: Is this service unique to NADZ?
items.a: Yes, NADZ Vital Brain is an exclusive offering, combining at-home QEEG brain mapping with AI-supported analytics and integrative package options.

items.q: Is the service private and confidential?
items.a: Yes. Testing and consultation are delivered discreetly, with privacy-first handling of your information.

items.q: Is this for emergencies?
items.a: If there are severe or sudden neurological symptoms (e.g., chest pain, fainting, stroke-like symptoms, seizures), seek emergency care immediately.

centeredText.title: What is POC Testing?
paragraphs: Point-of-Care (POC) testing refers to diagnostic tests performed at or near the patient, instead of sending samples to a lab and waiting for reports. These tests are designed to provide real-time results, helping clinicians and patients make quicker decisions.
POC testing is especially helpful for:
Rapid screening for common health concerns
Monitoring chronic conditions like diabetes
Identifying when lab confirmation or a doctor consult is required

centeredText2.title: Because You Deserve More Than a Waiting Room
paragraphs: When you are feeling unwell or want quick clarity, waiting for lab queues and next-day reports can feel unnecessary. With POC testing at home in Dubai, NADZ Home Healthcare brings rapid, medically validated screening to your location.

A DHA-licensed nurse (and doctor support when needed) arrives with MOHAP-certified point-of-care devices, performs the test safely, and shares results in minutes for many common checks like hemoglobin, blood sugar, HbA1c, WBC screening, and select rapid infection tests. If your results suggest follow-up is needed, we can coordinate doctor review and/or accredited lab testing right away.

centeredText.title: Feel Refreshed. Feel Rebalanced. Right Where You Are.
paragraphs: Travel fatigue, long work weeks, heat exposure, late nights, or simply running on empty can leave you feeling drained. NADZ Home Healthcare's IV Vitamin Therapy at home is designed to support hydration and replenishment in the comfort of your own space, without waiting rooms or traffic.

Instead of stepping out when you already don't feel your best, a NADZ clinical team comes to you. Your IV infusion is set up at your home, hotel, or office, you're monitored throughout, and you can rest while fluids and targeted vitamins are delivered directly into your bloodstream for efficient uptake.

howItWorks.title: How IV Drips at Home Work
howItWorks.description: Intravenous (IV) hydration delivers fluids directly into your vein, so they bypass the digestive system and enter your circulation more efficiently. A typical IV hydration session at home may contain a tailored mix of:
items: Hydration fluids and electrolytes for rehydration support
Vitamins and minerals to support recovery and vitality
Supportive add-ons, when clinically appropriate (for example, anti-nausea support)
howItWorks.closingParagraph: At NADZ, every drip is medically formulated and administered by DHA-certified clinicians. Before an infusion, your health history and goals are reviewed. During the session, you're monitored for comfort and safety, and you have a clear understanding of what's being infused and why.

faqs.title: FAQs – IV Vitamin Therapy at Home in Dubai

items.q: What is IV vitamin therapy?
items.a: It's a medically supervised IV infusion that may include fluids, electrolytes, and selected vitamins/minerals to support hydration and targeted wellness goals.

items.q: How long does an IV vitamin drip take?
items.a: Most sessions take around 30–60 minutes, depending on the protocol and your clinical assessment.

items.q: Is IV vitamin therapy safe?
items.a: When administered by licensed clinicians with proper screening and monitoring, it's generally considered safe for many people. NADZ will not proceed if it's not medically appropriate.

items.q: How soon will I feel the effects?
items.a: Some people feel refreshed during or shortly after the session, while others notice changes later. Results vary based on hydration level, baseline health, and the chosen drip.

items.q: Can I book IV vitamin therapy before travel or an important day?
items.a: In some cases, yes. Preventive scheduling may be appropriate for hydration support your clinician will advise based on your health profile.

items.q: Who should avoid IV vitamin therapy?
items.a: Suitability depends on your health conditions, symptoms, and medical history. If you have complex medical conditions, are pregnant/breastfeeding, or feel severely unwell, NADZ will guide you to the safest level of care.
